calendar.matters:
Petition For Order To Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James O'Neil, Esq., counsel for and with Eric Mayers, Successor Trustee (appearing separately by (appearing by video conference).

The Court notes at the outset that the current Petition before the Court, while not primarily designated a Probate Code Sec. 850 Petition, is, as a practical matter, a Probate Code Sec. 850 Petition. As those petitions are in rem proceedings, the Court does not believe it has jurisdiction to consider the status of the Texas or Nevada properties. Mr. O'Neil acknowledges the same, conceding that ancillary proceedings in the respective states will most likely be necessary to proceed with respect to those properties. The Court otherwise advises that it will take the matter under submission pending receipt of a revised Proposed Order removing the out-of-state properties and providing legal descriptions of the California properties. The revised Order must be received by the end of the week [August 28, 2020] or else the Court will vacate the under submission order and continue the hearing to a date to be determined.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition For Order To Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Continued To Aug-25-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil appeared by zoom for and with Eric Mayers by zoom.

The Court noted that the Assessor's parcel numbers on the updated schedules do not match what is on schedule A and questions how they know the street address for the Texas property. Mr. O'Neil stated that he did not prepare those schedules, that he is not sure where those numbers came from and that Eric helped his Mother review those schedules. Mr. O'Neil also stated that the street addresses are correct, that he researched those deeds online, and referred to Barbara signing the updated exhibit and the declaration connecting the addresses. The Court questions whether there is any legal authority from Texas and Nevada that California law is accepted for confirming real property to a trust. Mr. O'Neil stated that he has done some research, that Nevada has a Heggstad equivalent and that he was not able to find any analog for Texas. The Petition for Order to Confirm Assets to Revocable Trust is continued to August 25, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. O'Neil to file a declaration indicating what he did to research the parcel numbers and the addresses of the properties.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
